streams.reasoning: From Chemistry Plan stream_logic: dipolarophile in DES, kept separate to control stoichiometry at T-mixer before heated coil
From Chemistry Plan stream_logic: nitrile oxide precursor at 2× concentration of stream A to achieve 2.0 equiv at 2:1 flow rate ratio

mixing_order_reasoning: From Chemistry Plan: Both reagents mixed at T-mixer immediately before entering heated coil reactor. Rapid mixing ensures uniform stoichiometry. Nitrile oxide generated in situ only at elevated temperature, so premature dimerization at mixing point (room temperature) is minimal.
chemistry_notes: Thermal 1,3-dipolar cycloaddition driven by in-situ nitrile oxide generation from ethyl nitroacetate at 120°C. Flow advantages: (1) rapid heating in narrow-bore tubing eliminates thermal gradients in viscous DES that cause nitrile oxide dimerization; (2) precise residence time control prevents over-reaction; (3) BPR enables safe operation above solvent boiling point. Calculations show mass-transfer limitation (Da=39.4) requiring careful mixing design. High viscosity DES (~50-100 cP) handled via wider tubing (1.2mm ID from Analogy 3) and moderate flow rates.

reasoning_per_field.residence_time_min: Calculation estimated 0.8 min (range 0.5-2.2 min). Analogy 1 used 5 min for photocycloaddition at 200°C - chemically different (photochemical vs thermal) but similar cycloaddition mechanism. Using calculated 0.8 min as it matches batch time reduction target.
reasoning_per_field.flow_rate_mL_min: Calculation: total flow rate 0.5 mL/min to achieve 0.8 min residence time with 0.4 mL reactor volume. Stream A: 0.167 mL/min, Stream B: 0.333 mL/min (2:1 ratio for stoichiometry).
reasoning_per_field.temperature_C: Same as batch (120°C) - Chemistry Plan indicates thermal activation required for nitrile oxide generation. No temperature advantage needed.
reasoning_per_field.concentration_M: Calculation: reactor concentration 0.067 M after 1:2 dilution mixing. Lower than batch (0.5 M) but compensated by improved heat transfer.
reasoning_per_field.BPR_bar: Calculation: BPR required at 5.0 bar. Gas-liquid system detected - BPR mandatory to maintain gas solubility and prevent uncontrolled degassing. Analogy 1 used 34 bar, Analogy 2 used 47.6 bar - using calculated 5.0 bar as sufficient for this system.
reasoning_per_field.reactor_type: Coil reactor from Chemistry Plan - suitable for thermal reactions requiring uniform heating.
reasoning_per_field.tubing_material: PFA selected for high temperature operation (120°C) and chemical compatibility with DES. Superior to FEP at this temperature.
reasoning_per_field.tubing_ID_mm: 1.2 mm from Analogy 3 (similar flow rate 0.5 mL/min). Wider than calculated 1.0 mm to accommodate high viscosity DES and reduce pressure drop. Calculation showed Pe=9 (axial dispersion concern) - wider tubing acceptable.
reasoning_per_field.reactor_volume_mL: Calculation: 0.38 mL, rounded to 0.4 mL. Residence time = volume/flow rate = 0.4/0.5 = 0.8 min.
reasoning_per_field.light_setup: Empty string - Chemistry Plan confirms purely thermal reaction, no photochemical activation involved.
reasoning_per_field.wavelength_nm: Null - no light required for thermal cycloaddition reaction.
reasoning_per_field.deoxygenation_method: Null - Chemistry Plan confirms no oxygen sensitivity, reaction proceeds under air.
